pattern: Divide-and-conquer: a beautiful array can be split into odd-indexed values on the left and even-indexed values on the right; 2*mid is always even so it can never equal odd+even.
hints: Observe that if A is beautiful, then [2x-1 for x in A] (all odd) and [2x for x in A] (all even) are also beautiful, and concatenating odd+even preserves the property because odd+even is always odd, never 2*(middle).
Build the answer recursively: beautifulArray(n) = [scale-odd(beautifulArray(ceil(n/2)))] + [scale-even(beautifulArray(floor(n/2)))] then filter to keep only values in [1,n].
Use memoization keyed on the size to avoid recomputation. The recursion depth is O(log n) and produces a valid answer in O(n log n).

edge_inputs.description: n=1 — single element is trivially beautiful
n=3 — must find the unique no-AP permutation

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, max(1, size))]
